consistency.level_and_school: Consistent. Level 6 Divination spell fits the powerful effect of granting truesight.
consistency.components_and_consumption: Consistent. The material component explicitly states it is 'consumed by the spell,' which aligns with standard D&D 5e rules for consumed material costs.
consistency.effect_and_range: Consistent. The effect (truesight, seeing secret doors/ethereal plane) is appropriately powerful for a 6th-level spell, and the 120-foot range is a standard maximum range for focused personal enhancements.

quality.clarity: High. The description is clear, defining the specific abilities granted (truesight, secret door detection, ethereal vision) and the range.
quality.completeness: High. Includes all necessary mechanical elements: level, school, casting time, range, components (with cost/consumption noted), duration, description, and applicable classes.

mechanics.primary_mechanic: Grants the 'truesight' condition, which supersedes normal vision limitations up to 120 feet.
mechanics.resource_cost: One action for casting time; 25 gp material component consumed.
mechanics.duration_impact: A 1-hour duration makes this highly useful for scouting, investigation, or navigating complex, hidden environments.

analysis.notes: Mechanically sound and well-defined for a high-level utility/investigative spell. The material cost reinforces its significance.
